commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainers need to decide the Discord final-delivery privacy policy and require live Discord proof; automation should not choose whether to publish suppressed finals.; Needs attention: the diff can publish source-suppressed Discord final text or operational metadata into shared channels.; Review finding: [P1] Keep message-tool-only finals private by default — src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts:242 - [P2] Mark the... (sha=ecac27be7c2bdf77ab1b3d0853579c5b395ecc1d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Discord-specific `runReplyAgent` logic and e2e tests to synthesize or mark visible final payloads when a Discord turn lacks committed message-tool delivery evidence.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection of the PR head shows ordinary finals being marked with suppression-bypass metadata, while current main docs and tests define those finals as private. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the Discord final-delivery privacy policy and require live Discord proof; automation should not choose whether to publish suppressed finals.

target.title: perf(plugins): cut plugin-system thrash from TUI cold start
target.body: big improvement to plugin loading performance on gateway AND TUI (only TUI local once #84686 is merged) BEFORE AFTER ## Summary Cuts plugin-system work during TUI cold start. Freeze drops from ~32s to ~13–16s; residual cost is dominated by two single-plugin module loads (Codex 2.76s, Anthropic 746ms) that this PR doesn't touch. The work is four core fixes, ranked by single-fix impact: ### 1. Cache-busting — snapshot memo store/lookup keys now match (~7s) Previously the memo computed its lookup key from the persisted index and stored entries under the derived index. Those indexes always differ in volatile fields (`generatedAtMs`, `refreshReason`), so the keys never agreed and every lookup missed. **Hit rate goes from 24% → 98%.** Single biggest commit in the PR. ### 2. Plumbing — `PluginMetadataSnapshot` routed through resolve paths (~2–4s) `resolvePluginProviders`, `isPluginProvidersLoadInFlight`, `resolveOwningPluginIdsForProvider`, and `resolveExternalAuthProfilesWithPlugins` all dispatch to helpers that have a manifestRegistry fast path. They now thread the snapshot through so the fast path actually fires instead of every helper rebuilding the manifest registry from disk. `loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex` fires drop 685 → ~10. A second plumb threads `discovery?: PluginDiscoveryResult` through `applyPluginAutoEnable` → `detectPluginAutoEnableCandidates` → `resolvePluginAutoEnableReadiness` → `collectConfiguredChannelIds` → the bundled-channel leaf helpers, so a caller's discovery short-circuits the cascade. Only delivers value because (1) made the snapshot memo actually work. ### 3. LRU capacity (supporting) Memo was single-slot and thrashed when two stable input shapes alternated. Capacity-8 LRU keeps both. Necessary precondition for the cache-busting fix to land cleanly — without LRU, the memo evicts itself. ### 4. Eligibility predicate broadened (supporting) The memo previously only stored derived snapshots whose diagnostics were *exclusively* `persisted-registry-stale-policy`, which discarded ~98% of cold-start derives. Now stores every non-empty derived snapshot. To keep the invalidation surface honest, the cached entry's `watchedFiles` set is widened for derived snapshots to include the derived index's plugin manifest paths; the next lookup re-stats them and re-derives on any change. ## Verification - `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/providers.test.ts` — 93/93 pass (includes a new regression for derived-only manifest edits) - `pnpm build && pnpm openclaw tui` from a cold process — freeze 32s → 13–16s, snapshot memo hit rate 24% → 98%, per-plugin `package.json` reads ~914 → ~370 Not run: full `pnpm test` / `pnpm check`, sibling-process plugin-install interleaving. ## Notes - Three `wip(perf-trace):` commits are preserved in history so the audit instrumentation is easy to cherry-pick for future startup work. The chore() commit at the tip reverts their effects, so net diff against main contains zero `perfMark`/`perfSpan` calls. - An `installRecords` threading optimization was pulled into [`dallin-perf-install-records-threading`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/tree/dallin-perf-install-records-threading) for a follow-on PR — it cut ~264 sync disk reads but only ~140ms of freeze, so not worth bundling here.
